content:
- Completed the real kind-cluster verification for the k8s example, including /healthz, /readyz, /benchmark, /metrics, and a start/observe/stop benchmark cycle.
- Fixed the discovered PostgreSQL reserved-schema bug with a Red-Green test and centralized benchmark schema ownership in benchrunner.
- Required gates passed: make check, make lint, make test. Next I am staging all repo changes, committing with the task-finished message, and pushing.

- Read the prior codex progress log, active task summary, completed task summary, and the active task source files.
- Confirmed the actual repo stack is a Go service with cmd/internal packages, scratch Docker image, Docker Compose and Kubernetes examples, with remaining backlog in GitHub Actions, docs, static HTML UI, and final manual verification.
- Resolved blockers from .ralph/current_tasks_done.md: story-08/task-01 is now unblocked because story-07/task-01 passes true; all later active tasks remain blocked by this chain.
- No active unblocked bug tasks exist, and no priority tags outrank the current chain head.
- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt to .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr.md.
- Next, the implementation agent should complete the GHCR multi-arch GitHub Actions workflow, verify it properly, then re-run task selection.

- Read the prior codex progress log, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr.md.
- Next I am reading the task file and nearby story files to determine whether this turn is a plan-only pass or an execution pass.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is still in initial state with no plan attached yet.
- Determined this turn is planning-only per Ralph rules; execution must wait until a plan file is created and later promoted to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading existing delivery/release files so the workflow plan is specific and verification steps are realistic for this repository.

- Confirmed there is no existing .github workflow tree, so the active task will create the workflow from scratch.
- Reviewed the existing Dockerfile and Makefile to anchor the workflow plan around real repo validation and image-build behavior.
- Next I am checking git remote context and local tooling availability so the plan uses a verification path we can actually execute later.

- Confirmed local verification tooling exists for the future execution pass: actionlint, docker buildx, and docker manifest inspection commands are available.
- Reviewed task/plan conventions from prior Ralph tasks so I can patch the active task file with a matching <plan> entry.
- Next I am doing a final sweep for any existing release-story workflow notes, then I will create the plan file and update the task file to point at it.

- Created the active task plan at .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr_plans/2026-04-30-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr-plan.md.
- Updated .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr.md to point at that plan.
- The plan is planning-only, explicitly marks the workflow task as a TDD exception, applies improve-code-boundaries by separating validate/build/publish responsibilities, and ends in TO BE VERIFIED.
- Next turn should review the plan, switch its ending to NOW EXECUTE when satisfied, and then quit immediately per Ralph instructions.

- Read prior codex progress, the current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr.md and prior work created a planning-only file that still needs review.
- Next I am reading the task file and its referenced plan to decide whether I must quit after promoting the plan or can begin execution.

- Read the GHCR workflow task file and reviewed the referenced execution plan in full.
- The plan is sound: it keeps validation/build/publish boundaries clean, treats this workflow task as a TDD exception, and uses real verification via actionlint plus a pushed Actions run and manifest inspection.
- Promoted the plan terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE in .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr_plans/2026-04-30-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr-plan.md.
- Next turn should read the NOW EXECUTE plan, implement the workflow exactly as written, run make check/make lint/make test plus actionlint, then do the required remote workflow and manifest verification.

- Read prior progress, confirmed the active task pointer, and re-read the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill guidance.
- Confirmed prior turn already promoted the active plan to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should execute rather than create or review a plan.
- Next I am reading the active task file and referenced plan, then I will implement and verify the workflow per the plan.

- Read the active task file and full execution plan.
- Confirmed this task is a TDD exception and the execution target is a single GitHub Actions workflow with validate/build/publish boundaries and real remote verification.
- Next I am inspecting git status plus Dockerfile/Makefile/workflow tree so I can add the workflow without introducing boundary muddiness.

- Confirmed there is no existing workflow tree and the current Dockerfile/Makefile interface is simple enough for a clean validate/build/publish workflow.
- Verified official Docker/GitHub docs for GHCR token login, image exporter flags such as push-by-digest/name-canonical, and imagetools manifest creation.
- Next I am checking the improve-code-boundaries skill directory for additional smell guidance, then I will write the workflow file.

- Added .github/workflows/publish-ghcr.yml with separate validate, matrix build, and publish jobs.
- Kept the boundary clean by publishing per-platform images only by digest in build jobs and assigning the canonical ghcr.io/<repo>:<sha> tag only in publish.
- Next I am validating the workflow with actionlint and then running make check/make lint/make test.

- actionlint passed and the required local gates passed: make check, make lint, and make test.
- The remaining acceptance work is real GitHub verification: push a commit containing the workflow, confirm the Actions run graph/jobs, and inspect the published GHCR manifest for both platforms.
- Next I am checking the authenticated GitHub API wrapper usage, then I will make a temporary verification commit and push it.
